description_long: This object is a long, double-edged sword with a straight blade and a pointed tip, reminiscent of a European longsword. The blade appears to be made of dark metal, with subtle vertical lines suggesting a forged or patterned finish. The crossguard is simple and straight, extending horizontally and providing hand protection, while the handle is cylindrical and tapers slightly toward the pommel. The handle and crossguard are both dark in color, possibly made of metal or wood with a dark finish. There are no visible engravings, inlays, or decorative elements, giving the sword a utilitarian and functional appearance. The overall proportions suggest it is designed for two-handed use, suitable for display or as a prop in an urban or historical context.
description: A long, double-edged straight sword with a dark, unadorned blade, simple crossguard, and cylindrical handle. The design is functional and lacks ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard visible as a narrow line.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, straight, dark finish, no visible grip texture or ornamentation
